brief_justification: Paper A is a theoretical construction program (F-theory flux compactifications, moduli stabilization, algebraic-geometric toolkit, and counting/statistics of the string-landscape of vacua), whereas Paper B is observational statistical inference — likelihood fitting of the LCDM model's parameters (n_s, neutrino mass, N_eff, SZ amplitude) to CMB+BAO+H0 data. The only surface overlap is the word 'statistics', but landscape vacuum-counting and cosmological parameter estimation are different machineries, so no role-for-role mapping holds.
